evidence.scientific_clarity: 30-node, 4-community network rendered with spring_layout; community clusters are visually distinct, edge alpha=0.25 reduces hairball effect, node labels legible.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype or svg.fonttype; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type3 (bitmap glyphs), which is the matplotlib default — text is not editable/selectable.
evidence.palette: Four categorical colors (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) are not a formal colorblind-safe set; red and teal are borderline for deuteranopes, though no red/green directional confusion.
evidence.layout_density: Single panel fills the 7×7 inch canvas; tight_layout applied; no excessive whitespace or label overlaps observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately matches figure and explains algorithm, seed, and alpha; legend is present at lower-right; figure title in plot is redundant with caption but harmless; no specific numerical finding (e.g., edge count, cross-community fraction).
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses /Subtype /Type3 fonts (DejaVuSans, DejaVuSans-Bold) — confirms fonttype was NOT set to 42; text is non-selectable/non-editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All five expected files exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes layout algorithm, seed, edge transparency, and community color coding, and notes cross-community connectivity, but provides no concrete numerical claim (e.g., number of edges, modularity score, fraction of inter-community edges).

overall_strengths: Clean network visualization with well-separated communities, tuned node size and edge alpha, matching brief requirements.
All required output files are present; caption is detailed and algorithmically informative.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) not set, resulting in Type3 bitmap fonts in the PDF — text is non-selectable and the default DejaVu Sans typeface is used.
Palette is not a formally colorblind-safe set and the caption lacks a specific numerical finding to anchor the scientific claim.
